title: Artifact consumers determine repository boundaries
text: Artifact stream topology is the review surface for deciding whether produced artifacts should exist. A produced Artifact is appropriate only when a suitable Agent, Human, eval, handoff, or external delivery consumer exists in the workflow. Markdown/document artifacts without such a consumer should be omitted; when the content is meant for durable retrieval, query, replay, reasoning, or reuse, it should be structured as a machine-native artifact such as JSONL event_store, TTL/schema knowledge_store, or SQLite local_database. Directory structure is not the starting point; directories are reduced, merged, or retained according to workflow topology and artifact consumer fit.
author: user

metadata.criterion: Produced artifacts require an explicit Agent, Human, eval, handoff, or delivery consumer; otherwise omit or convert to an appropriate machine-native artifact.
